## SpaceX sets new Starship launch date; SPCX hits record post-IPO low while ASTS and RKLB rally

The intra-sector rotation — SPCX lower, ASTS and RKLB higher — is a meaningful signal; a PM with SPCX exposure should assess whether this is a temporary dislocation or a durable re-rating of relative positioning within the space economy theme as Starship execution risk is repriced.

- Following the launch abort, SpaceX announces a new Starship launch date.
- SPCX falls to a record post-IPO low on the combined news.
- Competing space names ASTS and RKLB rally in contrast — an apparent intra-sector rotation out of SPCX.
- The divergence suggests the market is differentiating execution risk within the space economy theme rather than selling the sector broadly.
